input.command:
gh pr create --title "Phase 1b — split @ax/credentials into facade + @ax/credentials-store-db" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Phase 1b of [`2026-04-27-agent-centric-simplification-design.md`](docs/plans/2026-04-27-agent-centric-simplification-design.md).

- New **`@ax/credentials-store-db`** plugin registers `credentials:store-blob:put` / `credentials:store-blob:get` and persists ciphertext blobs through the existing `storage:get` / `storage:set` surface under a `credential:` key prefix it owns.
- **`@ax/credentials`** is now a facade — encrypts via AES-256-GCM, validates ids, owns the empty-plaintext tombstone convention, and delegates persistence to the new store-blob seam. Consumer-facing `credentials:get` / `:set` / `:delete` hooks are unchanged in shape.
- The seam exists so future vault / KMS backends (`@ax/credentials-store-vault`, `…-aws-sm`, `…-gke-secret-manager`) drop in by registering `credentials:store-blob:*` against a real KMS — facade and consumers don't change.

## What's deferred (Phase 3 work, not Phase 1b)

- **Reshape consumer hooks** to `credentials:get(ref, { userId }) → string`, `credentials:set(ref, kind, blob, { userId, metadata })`, `credentials:list`, `credentials:rotate`. Phase 3 lands `kind` + per-kind dispatch (`credentials:resolve:anthropic-oauth`) when OAuth lifecycle needs them.
- **Dedicated `credentials` table with Kysely migrations** (`kind`, `expiresAt`, `metadata` columns). Today's store-blob delegates to `storage:*` — the seam is real (a vault backend wouldn't go through KV at all), but the table earns its weight in Phase 3.
- **`credentials:store-blob:delete`.** Tombstone-via-put still rides on `:put` to avoid a half-wired hook (Invariant 3). Lands when `storage:delete` does, or when a vault backend implements real deletion.

## Boundary review (per CLAUDE.md)

- **Alternate impl this hook could have:** `@ax/credentials-store-vault` registers `credentials:store-blob:*` against HashiCorp Vault; `@ax/credentials-store-aws-sm` against AWS Secrets Manager. Both bypass `storage:*` entirely.
- **Payload field names that might leak:** none. `id` and `blob` are opaque on the seam; the `credential:` storage-key prefix is internal to `@ax/credentials-store-db` and never appears in any hook payload.
- **Subscriber risk:** no subscribers today. A future audit-log subscriber on `credentials:store-blob:get` would see only `id` (vendor-neutral) and the encrypted blob — no plaintext path through this layer.
- **Wire surface:** none. `credentials:store-blob:*` is host-side only; the IPC server doesn't route `credentials:*` actions across the sandbox boundary.

## The five invariants

| | |
|---|---|
| I1 transport/storage-agnostic | ✅ Seam is bytes in / bytes out; no backend vocab in payloads. |
| I2 no cross-plugin imports | ✅ Facade calls store-blob via the bus. Test imports of sibling plugins are exempt per `eslint.config.mjs`. |
| I3 no half-wired plugins | ✅ Store-db is loaded by CLI + k8s preset. The two registered hooks (`:put`, `:get`) both have live callers. `:delete` deferred to Phase 3 (not registered, not unused). |
| I4 one source of truth | ✅ Store-db owns the `credential:` key prefix; facade owns encryption + tombstone semantic. No duplication. |
| I5 capabilities minimized | ✅ Store-db has no new capabilities — it's strictly less reach than what the facade had before. No fs / net / process / env added. |

## What landed

**New (1 package):**
- `@ax/credentials-store-db` — 7 source files, 10 tests, SECURITY.md.

**Modified:**
- `@ax/credentials` — facade refactor (`storage:*` → `credentials:store-blob:*`); same consumer hooks.
- `@ax/cli` `main.ts` + `credentials.ts` + `mcp.ts` subcommands — load `createCredentialsStoreDbPlugin()` before `createCredentialsPlugin()`.
- `@ax/preset-k8s` — same wiring, plus tsconfig + package.json refs.
- 7 test stacks bootstrap the new plugin (credentials × 1, credentials-store-db × 1 new file, cli × 4, mcp-client × 2, k8s preset).
- Root `tsconfig.json` reference for the new package (per Phase 1a memory note).

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm build` clean (root `tsc --build`).
- [x] `pnpm lint` clean.
- [x] `pnpm test` — 1100+ tests across 45 packages all pass, including the 10 new `@ax/credentials-store-db` tests.
- [x] `@ax/credentials`'s 16 existing tests pass against the new facade-over-store-blob flow (round-trip, missing id, delete, error redaction, tampered ciphertext).
- [x] Each commit leaves the tree green: commit 1 is purely additive; commit 2 swaps the facade and wires the new plugin into every consumer in the same change.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

---
name: Phase 1b (credentials facade + store-db split) shipped
description: 2026-04-28 ship of @ax/credentials-store-db + @ax/credentials facade refactor. Consumer-facing hooks unchanged. Reshape deferred to Phase 3.
type: project
---
Shipped 2026-04-28 as PR #19 against `phase-1b-credentials-split` branch (2 commits: scaffold + refactor).

Plan: `docs/plans/2026-04-27-agent-centric-simplification-design.md` Section 7 Phase 1b.

**What landed:**
- New `@ax/credentials-store-db` (10 tests). Registers `credentials:store-blob:put` / `credentials:store-blob:get`. Calls `storage:get` / `storage:set` under a `credential:` key prefix it owns.
- `@ax/credentials` refactored to call store-blob seam. Encryption + tombstone semantic stay in facade.
- Consumer-facing hooks (`credentials:get` / `:set` / `:delete`) **unchanged in shape** — the project_phase_1a_shipped.md prediction that 1b would reshape `credentials:get` was wrong. Reshape deferred to Phase 3 when OAuth lifecycle actually needs `kind` / `userId` / `resolve:<kind>`.
- Wired into `@ax/cli` (main + commands) and `@ax/preset-k8s`. Credential-proxy's call sites needed NO changes.

**Scope decision (the one judgment call):**

The plan's Phase 1b text said "Behavior identical; consumer-facing hooks unchanged" — Section 3 of the same design specified a redesigned shape with `kind`/`userId`/`metadata`/`list`/`rotate`. I flagged the ambiguity to the user (option A: minimal mechanical refactor; option B: faithful to Section 3). User picked **A**.

Rationale: doing reshape speculatively in 1b would churn all call sites with no per-kind benefit yet. Phase 3 owns the reshape when `credentials:resolve:anthropic-oauth` arrives.

**Two follow-on consequences for future phases:**

1. **Phase 3 must reshape `credentials:get`** — add `kind` parameter, return string directly (vs. current `{value}`). Will touch credential-proxy's two call sites + mcp-client's transport layer + CLI commands. Same "verbatim or translation layer" split that the Phase 1a memory called out — except now we know it's NOT verbatim because Phase 3 introduces real semantic change.

2. **`credentials:store-blob:delete` not yet registered** — facade still uses encrypted-empty-string tombstone via `:put`. Lands when `storage:delete` lands or a vault backend implements real deletion.

**Half-wired window now closed for Phase 1a too:** PR #19 wires `@ax/credentials-store-db` but does NOT load `@ax/credential-proxy` in the CLI or k8s preset (those are Phase 2's job). Phase 2 is now the only outstanding half-wired window from Phase 1a. 1-week clock that started 2026-04-28 still ticking.

**Stats:** 8 new files (1 new package), 19 modified files. ~547 lines added across all commits. 10 new tests + 1100+ existing tests pass.

**Storage backend dialect-agnostic question:** the design's Section 3 says credentials-store-db is "dialect-agnostic via `database:get-instance`" mirroring the storage-{sqlite,postgres} pattern. We deferred that — Phase 1b's store-db just delegates to `storage:get`/`storage:set`. When a real Phase 3 schema (`kind`, `expiresAt`, `metadata` columns) lands, we'll either build `@ax/database-sqlite` (doesn't exist) or have store-db open its own better-sqlite3 connection (mirror of storage-sqlite). Decision deferred until the schema earns its weight.

Plan: `docs/plans/2026-04-27-agent-centric-simplification-design.md` Section 7 Phase 1b.

**What landed:**
- New `@ax/credentials-store-db` (10 tests). Registers `credentials:store-blob:put` / `credentials:store-blob:get`. Calls `storage:get` / `storage:set` under a `credential:` key prefix it owns.
- `@ax/credentials` refactored to call store-blob seam. Encryption + tombstone semantic stay in facade.
- Consumer-facing hooks (`credentials:get` / `:set` / `:delete`) **unchanged in shape** — the project_phase_1a_shipped.md prediction that 1b would reshape `credentials:get` was wrong. Reshape deferred to Phase 3 when OAuth lifecycle actually needs `kind` / `userId` / `resolve:<kind>`.
- Wired into `@ax/cli` (main + commands) and `@ax/preset-k8s`. Credential-proxy's call sites needed NO changes.

**Scope decision (the one judgment call):**

The plan's Phase 1b text said "Behavior identical; consumer-facing hooks unchanged" — Section 3 of the same design specified a redesigned shape with `kind`/`userId`/`metadata`/`list`/`rotate`. I flagged the ambiguity to the user (option A: minimal mechanical refactor; option B: faithful to Section 3). User picked **A**.

Rationale: doing reshape speculatively in 1b would churn all call sites with no per-kind benefit yet. Phase 3 owns the reshape when `credentials:resolve:anthropic-oauth` arrives.

**Two follow-on consequences for future phases:**

1. **Phase 3 must reshape `credentials:get`** — add `kind` parameter, return string directly (vs. current `{value}`). Will touch credential-proxy's two call sites + mcp-client's transport layer + CLI commands. Same "verbatim or translation layer" split that the Phase 1a memory called out — except now we know it's NOT verbatim because Phase 3 introduces real semantic change.

2. **`credentials:store-blob:delete` not yet registered** — facade still uses encrypted-empty-string tombstone via `:put`. Lands when `storage:delete` lands or a vault backend implements real deletion.

**Half-wired window now closed for Phase 1a too:** PR #19 wires `@ax/credentials-store-db` but does NOT load `@ax/credential-proxy` in the CLI or k8s preset (those are Phase 2's job). Phase 2 is now the only outstanding half-wired window from Phase 1a. 1-week clock that started 2026-04-28 still ticking.

**Stats:** 8 new files (1 new package), 19 modified files. ~547 lines added across all commits. 10 new tests + 1100+ existing tests pass.

**Storage backend dialect-agnostic question:** the design's Section 3 says credentials-store-db is "dialect-agnostic via `database:get-instance`" mirroring the storage-{sqlite,postgres} pattern. We deferred that — Phase 1b's store-db just delegates to `storage:get`/`storage:set`. When a real Phase 3 schema (`kind`, `expiresAt`, `metadata` columns) lands, we'll either build `@ax/database-sqlite` (doesn't exist) or have store-db open its own better-sqlite3 connection (mirror of storage-sqlite). Decision deferred until the schema earns its weight.

Shipped 2026-04-28 as fast-forward merge to main, range `9609b8e..4cdcc79` (26 commits). Pushed to origin.

Plan: `docs/plans/2026-04-27-phase-1a-credential-proxy-impl.md`. PR notes: `docs/plans/2026-04-27-phase-1a-pr-notes.md`. Security note: `docs/plans/2026-04-27-phase-1a-security-note.md`.

**What landed (2 new packages, ~2200 LOC impl + ~3000 LOC tests, 76 new tests):**
- `@ax/credential-proxy` (host-side plugin) — registers `proxy:open-session` / `proxy:rotate-session` / `proxy:close-session`; calls `credentials:get`; fires `event.http-egress`. MITM listener with TLS termination + credential substitution + canary scan + per-session allowlist + private-IP block (incl. IPv4-mapped IPv6 unwrap, Azure IMDS 168.63/16, CGNAT 100.64/10) + DNS rebinding defense + CA management.
- `@ax/credential-proxy-bridge` (sandbox-side library, no `@ax/core` dep) — `startWebProxyBridge(unixSocketPath) → { port, stop }`. TCP↔Unix-socket relay for k8s. Pure relay, zero policy.

**KEY for Phase 1b:**

1. **`@ax/credential-proxy` calls `credentials:get` with the CURRENT `({id}) → {value}` shape** (not the design's eventual `(ref, {userId}) → currentValue`). See `packages/credential-proxy/src/plugin.ts` `proxy:open-session` + `proxy:rotate-session` handlers. Phase 1b must update BOTH the credentials plugin AND the proxy's call sites in the same PR (verbatim if reshape doesn't change semantic, or with a translation layer if shape changes are non-trivial).

2. **Phase 1a is half-wired** — both new plugins are integration-tested but NOT loaded by `cli/main.ts`. Phase 2 wires them into `agent-claude-sdk-runner` + CLI. CLAUDE.md tension was flagged in Phase 1a PR notes; Phase 2 needs to land within ~1 week to close the window (1-week clock started 2026-04-28).

3. **Tests in proxy use a `memCredentialsPlugin()` stub** (in `packages/credential-proxy/src/__tests__/plugin.test.ts` + `egress-events.test.ts` + `acceptance.test.ts` + `integration-with-bridge.test.ts`). Phase 1b should refactor this stub to match the new credentials hook shape.

**Plugins added to root `tsconfig.json` references** (the implementer originally missed this; root tsc --build silently skipped them). Watch for this pattern in future new-plugin PRs.

**Deferred follow-ups (don't block but track):**
- Tighter pin on `node-forge` (currently `^1.3.1`) — credential-proxy is security-critical.
- Corrupt-CA-on-disk recovery (v1 silently regenerates — arguably wrong).
- Chunk-boundary substitution: `replaceAllBuffer` is buffer-level; placeholder split across 2 TCP chunks leaks literal `ax-cred:<hex>` to upstream (no security impact, just usability — upstream returns 401). Same for canary scan.
- `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` sweep — fixed for credential-proxy in this PR; other packages may have latent issues if they're not in root tsconfig refs.

**Invariants verified in Phase 1a (from impl plan I1-I9):** all green. See PR notes for the verification table.

# Decisions

Architectural / process decisions. Never deleted — strikethrough if reversed.

| Date | Decision | Rationale | Alternatives |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2026-04-23 | Ship `storage:get` / `storage:set` without `database:get-instance` abstraction | YAGNI until a second backend exists; architecture doc Section 6 defers this until `@ax/storage-postgres` lands | Introduce the abstraction now (rejected: premature, no second impl to validate shape) |
| 2026-04-23 | Ship `storage:get` without a runtime consumer in Week 3 | Symmetric API with `storage:set`; consumer (session lookup) lands Week 4+. Documented waiver in PR #2 | Wait until Week 4 to register `storage:get` (rejected: half-wired feels worse than a one-slice-early symmetric pair) |
| 2026-04-23 | `@ax/audit-log` owns the `chat:<reqId>` key namespace in storage | One-source-of-truth invariant — any other plugin wanting to read audit records does so through a future service hook, not by reading the key directly | Shared key convention across plugins (rejected: violates invariant #4) |

Phase 1b shipped as **PR #19**: https://github.com/project-ax/ax-next/pull/19

**Summary:**
- New `@ax/credentials-store-db` plugin registers `credentials:store-blob:put/get`, owns the `credential:` storage-key prefix.
- `@ax/credentials` is now a facade calling the store-blob seam; encryption + tombstone semantic stay in facade; consumer hooks unchanged.
- Wired into `@ax/cli` and `@ax/preset-k8s`. 7 test stacks updated.
- Two commits, each leaves the tree green. 1100+ tests pass; lint + build clean.

Memory updated: added `project_phase_1b_shipped.md`, corrected the Phase 1a memory's outdated reshape prediction, and recorded the two scope decisions in repo `decisions.md`.

Phase 2 (wiring `@ax/credential-proxy` + bridge into `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` and CLI) is the next outstanding half-wired window from Phase 1a — 1-week clock still ticking from 2026-04-28.

---
rules:
  # No eval() anywhere in application code.
  - id: ax-no-eval
    pattern: eval(...)
    message: >
      eval() is never permitted in AX application code. Use safe
      alternatives instead.
    languages: [typescript]
    severity: ERROR
    paths:
      include:
        - /packages/

  # No Function() constructor (eval equivalent).
  - id: ax-no-function-constructor
    pattern: new Function(...)
    message: >
      new Function() is an eval equivalent and is never permitted in AX.
    languages: [typescript]
    severity: ERROR
    paths:
      include:
        - /packages/
rules:
  # CI-only: flag console.log in plugin code (should use createLogger from @ax/core).
  # Per-package `scripts/` trees are exempt: they are manual / smoke scripts
  # invoked by hand (not plugin runtime code), so routing their output through
  # a structured logger would defeat the point of running them.
  - id: ax-no-console-in-plugins
    pattern: console.log(...)
    message: >
      Use createLogger() from @ax/core instead of console.log() in plugin
      code. console.log() is acceptable in CLI code if/when a CLI plugin
      lands — add an exclude here at that point.
    languages: [typescript]
    severity: WARNING
    paths:
      include:
        - /packages/
      exclude:
        - /packages/*/scripts/

  # CI-only: catch __proto__ or constructor.prototype pollution vectors.
  - id: ax-no-prototype-pollution
    patterns:
      - pattern-either:
          - pattern: $OBJ["__proto__"]
          - pattern: $OBJ.__proto__
          - pattern: $OBJ["constructor"]["prototype"]
    message: >
      Potential prototype pollution vector detected. Ensure user-controlled
      keys cannot modify Object.prototype.
    languages: [typescript]
    severity: ERROR
    paths:
      include:
        - /packages/

content.text:
Three things to fix. Plan:
1. **Semgrep findings** in 3 credential-proxy test files (Phase 1a leftover, false-positives on test code that exercises the proxy itself) — add `// nosemgrep:rule-id` annotations with justification.
2. **Cookie tamper test** in k8s preset (genuine flake — base64url last-char swap doesn't always change decoded HMAC bytes due to padding) — flip a middle char of the signature instead.
3. **CodeRabbit nitpicks** in credentials-store-db tests — extract bootstrap helper + tighten error code assertions.

Starting with the cookie tamper bug (real bug):

lines: it('B. Cookie tamper: flipping one base64url char in the HMAC segment → 401', async () => {
-    // Flip the last character. The base64url alphabet only matters in
-    // that we stay legal; both 'A' and 'B' are valid, so the swap
-    // doesn't trip the parser before the HMAC check runs.
+    // Flip a middle character of the signature, NOT the last one.
+    // Why not the last char: 32 bytes of HMAC is 256 bits but the
+    // base64url encoding takes 43 chars × 6 bits = 258 bits — the
+    // bottom 2 bits of the last char are padding/discarded. So
+    // bits and the decoded HMAC bytes are identical → verification
+    // succeeds → test sometimes saw 200 instead of 401 (~6% flake
+    // rate when the random HMAC's last char happens to be A/B/C/D).
+    // The signature is the last 43 chars (HMAC-SHA256 base64url); the
+    // 22nd-from-end char is well inside the signature payload bits
+    // and any bit-flip there changes a real byte in the decoded HMAC.
+    // Pick a swap that's guaranteed to flip at least one of the top
+    // 4 bits — `A` and `Q` differ only in bit 4 (A=000000, Q=010000),
+    // both base64url-legal so we stay in alphabet.

input.command:
git add presets/k8s/src/__tests__/multi-tenant-acceptance.test.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(presets-k8s): cookie-tamper test base64-padding flake

The "B. Cookie tamper: flipping one base64url char in the HMAC segment → 401"
test was flipping the LAST char of the cookie value. HMAC-SHA256 is 32 bytes
(256 bits) but base64url-encoded as 43 chars (43 × 6 = 258 bits) — the
bottom 2 bits of the last char are padding/discarded by the decoder.

Concretely: `A` (000000) and `B` (000001) decode to the same final byte
because they differ only in the discarded bits. So when the random HMAC
happened to end in A/B/C/D (probability ~6/64), the test's
`A↔B` swap left the decoded HMAC unchanged and the tampered cookie
verified as valid → 200 instead of expected 401.

Fix: flip a middle char of the signature (position -22, well inside the
real-bit region). Use `A`↔`Q` swap (differ in bit 4) so even if the
target char happens to be A/Q, the swap still flips a real bit.

Pre-existing flake — not introduced by Phase 1b but blocking PR #19's CI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

chore(tests): silence false-positive semgrep rules in fixture code

Four pre-existing semgrep findings in test fixtures, all false positives:

1. credential-proxy/src/__tests__/listener-http.test.ts:122 —
   `fetch('http://127.0.0.1/')`. The whole test asserts the proxy's
   private-IP block fires; HTTPS would change the path under test.

2. credential-proxy/src/__tests__/egress-events.test.ts:431 —
   same: testing private-IP block + event.http-egress shape.

3. credential-proxy/src/__tests__/listener-connect-bypass.test.ts:124 —
   `tlsConnect({ rejectUnauthorized: false })` to a self-signed test
   server we spawned in this very test. The test verifies CONNECT
   bypass mode delivers raw bytes without MITM termination.

4. ipc-http/src/__tests__/listener.test.ts:145 — `Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz`
   is the canonical RFC 7617 example string ("user:pass"), used to
   prove the listener rejects non-Bearer schemes with 401.

Inline `// nosemgrep:RULE_ID` annotations with reasoning. Pre-existing
findings on main; not introduced by Phase 1b but blocking PR #19's CI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

test(credentials-store-db): tighten + dedupe per CodeRabbit (Phase 1b)

Two CodeRabbit nitpicks on PR #19:

- Extract repeated `HookBus + bootstrap` setup into
  `bootstrapWithMemStore()` helper. Removes ~50 lines of duplicated
  three-line bootstrap blocks across 8 test cases.
- Tighten error-class assertions: tests that previously asserted

  validation behavior, not just "anything that throws PluginError."

Both are quality-of-life cleanups; no behavior change.

content.text:
All checks pass. PR #19 is now green.

**Final state:**
- `test` ✅ pass (4m5s)
- `semgrep` ✅ pass (42s, was failing on 3 findings)
- `gitleaks` ✅ pass
- `Analyze (javascript-typescript)` ✅ pass
- `CodeRabbit` ✅ pass

**Three follow-up commits added to PR #19:**
- `9ffe987` fix(presets-k8s): cookie-tamper test base64-padding flake — the test was flipping the *last* char of the cookie, but base64url's last 6-bit position has 2 padding bits that the decoder discards, so `A↔B` swaps yielded identical decoded HMAC bytes ~6% of the time. Now flips a middle char with a 4-bit difference.
- `38d74d3` chore(tests): silence false-positive semgrep rules in fixture code — 4 inline `// nosemgrep:RULE_ID` annotations with reasoning, on test code that exercises HTTP/TLS or basic-auth paths the proxy/listener under test must intentionally accept.
- `15b8e7a` test(credentials-store-db): tighten + dedupe per CodeRabbit — extracted `bootstrapWithMemStore()` helper (-50 lines duplication) and tightened error assertions from `toBeInstanceOf(PluginError)` to `toMatchObject({ code: 'invalid-payload' })`.

The cookie-tamper bug and the semgrep findings were Phase 1a leftovers blocking the PR's CI, not Phase 1b changes — fixed here so PR #19 can land green. Memory updated with the cookie-padding decision.
